rawContent: Task: 842-260409-112718 | Outcome: completed | Observation: Task 842-260409-112718 completed a chore to verify existing MCP server integration implementation, with all 7 pipeline stages completing successfully including verify which required 1 retry. However, the verify.md artifact shows verification FAIL due to persistent PagesPageConfig type constraint errors in .next/dev/types/validator.ts (lines 350, 386, 395) that appear to be structural page configuration mismatches. The pipeline nonetheless proceeded to ship stage and updated PR #843, continuing the established pattern where typecheck failures do not block deployment. | Pattern: This is the fifth consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, and now 842) exhibiting the identical pattern: verify stage reports FAIL due to PagesPageConfig type constraint violations in .next/dev/types/validator.ts, yet the pipeline proceeds through review-fix and ship without blocking. Four prior retrospectives recommended adding a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status, but the flaw persists unchanged. The autofix stage cannot resolve this class of structural type errors, and the pipeline lacks a mechanism to halt when verify reports FAIL. | Suggestion: Since four consecutive retrospectives have recommended implementing a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status without implementation, escalate this to a configuration change rather than another recommendation: add a verifyExitCode check in the ship stage template that reads the verify stage's exit status and halts with an error when verify reports FAIL, preventing automatic shipment of code that failed typecheck validation. | Flaw: verify — Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage | Stages: taskify:completed, plan:completed, build:completed, verify:completed, review:completed, review-fix:completed, ship:completed
